verdict_reason: BG 2.67's tags are focus-practice, deep-work, engineering-pathology. CROSS-REFERENCE WITH SAME-TAG VERSES IN AVAILABLE CORPUS: (a) engineering-pathology is shared with 2.46 ('the senior engineer no longer reads the linter; the linter is reading her code' — premature-mastery pathology). 2.67 teaches input-stream-drift as the prajñā-thief; 2.46 teaches premature-mastery / rule-amnesia. These are DIFFERENT engineering pathologies but both are named, falsifiable, and operationally describable — coherent under a doctrine of 'engineering pathologies are nameable structural failure modes'. No drift detected. (b) focus-practice and deep-work are likely shared with parallel-agent-generated verses 2.58-2.61 (sense-control block) and the chapter's tortoise-verse area; without those verses available, doctrine-coherence on these tags is to-be-checked at chapter assembly. The engineering layer for 2.67 takes the prajñā-erosion stance which is compatible with sense-control verses' positive prescriptions. CROSS-REFERENCES: 2.67 makes one cross-reference to 2.68 ('the negative case-statement immediately preceding 2.68's positive corollary') which is structurally correct and supported by the source pack's traditional_meaning_consensus. Pass on both 6.1 (compatible doctrine across same-tag verses) and 6.5 (cross-reference is supported by the cited verse's traditional meaning).
